core_mechanism: Proof of existence, uniqueness, and exponentially fast convergence to a unique invariant (stationary) probability measure for a noise-driven dynamical system in which randomness enters through only a few degrees of freedom, using Hörmander's bracket/controllability (hypoellipticity) condition to show the noise propagates to the entire state space
brief_reason: The abstract states that the non-equilibrium steady state can be shown to exist and be unique with exponentially fast convergence using the Hörmander conditions and controllability, with Malliavin calculus giving regularity — i.e. hypoellipticity/controllability arguments for a unique, exponentially-attracting stationary measure of a degenerately-forced stochastic system.
